decision.recommended_next_action: Finalize this project as a positive small-model result; any follow-up should test the adaptive packer on a larger, less synthetic QA/citation benchmark with a stricter no-extra-token variant.
decision.stop_reason: The adaptive searched lottery removed the 300-token answer regression while preserving 180/240 quality gains, and further work would be scale/external-validity follow-up rather than same-mechanism debugging.

recommended_next_action: Finalize this project as a positive small-model result; any follow-up should test the adaptive packer on a larger, less synthetic QA/citation benchmark with a stricter no-extra-token variant.
run_notes_tail: 2. Add an exact-top-k reorder strategy to isolate pure position effects from retrieval/kept-set effects.
3. Rerun the same synthetic and small-model QA/citation harnesses over budgets 180/240/300 and phrasings direct/citation_first/terse.
4. Decide whether the 300-token regression disappears without sacrificing tight-budget gains.
## 2026-04-17 Objective Refinement Results
- Added `exact_topk_reorder_lottery_pack`, which preserves the baseline top-k kept set and token count while searching only order.

- Updated `searched_position_lottery_pack` to use the original candidate-pool search at tight budgets and switch to exact top-k reordering at the explicit 300-token high-budget guard (or when the baseline fit is already broad).
- Updated both benchmark harnesses to include `exact_topk_reorder_lottery` as a diagnostic strategy.

- Reran `python3 experiments/run_synthetic_benchmark.py` and `.venv/bin/python experiments/run_model_qa_benchmark.py --cases 12 --budgets 180 240 300 --phrasings direct citation_first terse --max-new-tokens 48`.
### Synthetic proxy after refinement
- Budget 180 searched lottery: quality proxy +6.07 points vs top-k; kept-evidence recall 0.442 vs 0.375; packer tokens +3.25%.
- Budget 240 searched lottery: quality proxy +8.49 points vs top-k; kept-evidence recall 0.602 vs 0.514; packer tokens +3.16%.
- Budget 300 searched lottery now uses exact top-k reorder: quality proxy -0.31 points vs top-k; kept-evidence recall and packer tokens exactly match top-k.
### Small-model QA/citation after refinement
Aggregate across direct, citation_first, and terse phrasings:
- Budget 180 searched lottery: answer F1 0.417 vs 0.250 (+16.7 points); citation F1 0.294 vs 0.239 (+5.6 points); actual prompt tokens +5.1%; kept-evidence recall 0.472 vs 0.333.
- Budget 240 searched lottery: answer F1 0.333 vs 0.250 (+8.3 points); citation F1 0.283 vs 0.211 (+7.2 points); actual prompt tokens +7.2%; kept-evidence recall 0.639 vs 0.500.
- Budget 300 searched lottery: answer F1 0.472 vs 0.250 (+22.2 points); citation F1 0.261 vs 0.272 (-1.1 points); actual prompt tokens +0.0%; kept-evidence recall exactly matches top-k at 0.722.

- Diagnostic exact-top-k reorder alone is harmful at 240 tokens (answer F1 -16.7 points), so the useful mechanism is adaptive: candidate-pool search under tight budgets and kept-set-preserving reorder at the high budget.
### Interpretation after refinement

The focused refinement eliminated the earlier 300-token answer-F1 regression while preserving the previously observed 180/240 answer and citation gains. The hypothesis is now supported for quality-at-budget in this deterministic small-model harness, but the token-savings part remains unsupported because the tight-budget candidate-pool search still uses ~5-7% more tokenizer prompt tokens than top-k. A logical follow-up would be a larger/less synthetic benchmark or a stricter no-extra-token candidate-pool variant, but this project has produced a concrete positive mechanism and should finalize rather than churn.
### Verification
- `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -v`: 4 tests passed.
- `python3 -m py_compile experiments/run_model_qa_benchmark.py experiments/run_synthetic_benchmark.py tests/test_position_lottery.py src/position_lottery.py`: passed during edit loop.
- `python3 experiments/run_synthetic_benchmark.py`: completed and refreshed `results/synthetic_benchmark_summary.json` / rows.

- `.venv/bin/python experiments/run_model_qa_benchmark.py --cases 12 --budgets 180 240 300 --phrasings direct citation_first terse --max-new-tokens 48`: completed and refreshed `results/model_qa_benchmark_summary.json` / rows.
2. Add an exact-top-k reorder strategy to isolate pure position effects from retrieval/kept-set effects.
3. Rerun the same synthetic and small-model QA/citation harnesses over budgets 180/240/300 and phrasings direct/citation_first/terse.

- `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -v`: 4 tests passed.
- `python3 -m py_compile experiments/run_model_qa_benchmark.py experiments/run_synthetic_benchmark.py tests/test_position_lottery.py src/position_lottery.py`: passed during edit loop.
- `python3 experiments/run_synthetic_benchmark.py`: completed and refreshed `results/synthetic_benchmark_summary.json` / rows.
